solve_notes: Added `checkArgument(minLength >= 0, "invalid minLength: %s", minLength)` after the existing `checkNotNull(string)` in both `padStart()` and `padEnd()`. Also updated their Javadoc to remove the 'Can be zero or negative' statement and added `@throws IllegalArgumentException if minLength is negative`. The error-message format mirrors how `Strings.repeat()` validates its `count` parameter.
task_text:
Fix Strings.padStart and Strings.padEnd not validating negative minLength

Both `Strings.padStart(String, int, char)` and `Strings.padEnd(String,
int, char)` currently document and accept negative `minLength` values,
silently returning the original string — for example, the Javadoc says
"Can be zero or negative, in which case the input string is always
returned." This differs from `Strings.repeat(String, int)`, which
throws `IllegalArgumentException` for a negative `count`. Fix both
`padStart()` and `padEnd()` in `Strings.java` to throw
`IllegalArgumentException` when `minLength < 0`, and update their
Javadoc to remove the statement that negative values are allowed,
making them consistent with `Strings.repeat()`.

The repeat() method is added to thrash_preventing because an AI agent would look it up to confirm the exact error-message format ('invalid count: %s' vs. other possibilities) before writing the analogous check, preventing it from guessing and possibly producing an inconsistent message.
